title: Fix Client Create Save and Inventory Create/Edit — Full CRUD & Blob Storage Repair

requirements.summary: Fix ClientDetailPage.tsx create mode to properly call createClient and navigate on success
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Save'/'Create' on a new client form calls the backend createClient endpoint with the correct arguments.
On successful createClient call, the user is navigated back to '/clients'.
Error states are surfaced to the user if createClient fails.
The Save button is never in a permanently disabled state in create mode.
event.preventDefault() is called to prevent unintended page reloads.

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the form submission handler for create mode. Ensure event.preventDefault() is called at the top of the handler. Verify the createClient mutation from useQueries is correctly invoked with all form field values (name, email, phone, address, notes, googleReviewUrl). Confirm the mutation's onSuccess callback navigates to '/clients' using the router. Remove any logic that permanently disables the Save button in create mode. Add visible error feedback if createClient fails. Verify the create client mutation hook in useQueries.ts is properly calling actor.createClient() before implementing.

requirements.summary: Fix InventoryPage.tsx 'Add Part' inline form to properly call createPart and refresh the list
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the submit/save button on the Add Part inline form calls the backend createPart endpoint with all entered field values.
The parts list refreshes/updates after a successful createPart call.
Error states are surfaced if createPart fails.
The submit button is not disabled or a no-op in add mode.

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the Add Part inline form submission handler. Ensure the form's onSubmit or button's onClick properly prevents default behavior and calls the createPart mutation from useQueries with all entered fields (name, partNumber, description, quantityOnHand, unitCost, lowStockThreshold if present). Confirm the mutation's onSuccess callback triggers a query invalidation or refetch to update the parts list. Add visible error feedback if createPart fails. Ensure the submit button is enabled and functional in add mode. Verify the create part mutation hook in useQueries.ts is properly calling actor.createPart() before implementing.

requirements.summary: Fix InventoryPage.tsx edit and delete flows to properly call updatePart and deletePart
acceptanceCriteria: Saving an edited part calls updatePart with the correct id and updated fields.
Deleting a part calls deletePart with the correct id and removes it from the list.
Success and error states are visibly communicated to the user.

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the edit-part save handler. Ensure the updatePart mutation from useQueries is called with the correct part id and all updated field values. Confirm the mutation's onSuccess callback updates the UI (invalidates queries or closes the edit dialog). Audit and fix the delete-part handler to call the deletePart mutation with the correct part id. Ensure both mutations provide visible success/error feedback to the user. Verify the update and delete part mutation hooks in useQueries.ts are properly calling actor.updatePart() and actor.deletePart() before implementing.

requirements.summary: Audit and fix useQueries.ts mutation hooks for createClient, createPart, updatePart, deletePart, addJobPhoto, removeJobPhoto, and storeUserSignature
acceptanceCriteria: Each mutation hook in useQueries.ts calls the correct actor method with the correct argument shape.
The actor reference is resolved (not null/undefined) before the mutation body executes.
Mutations for createClient, createPart, updatePart, deletePart, addJobPhoto, removeJobPhoto, and storeUserSignature are all functional and non-no-op.

file_operations.description: Audit each mutation hook (useCreateClient, useCreatePart, useUpdatePart, useDeletePart, useAddJobPhoto, useRemoveJobPhoto, useStoreUserSignature). For each mutation, verify: 1) the actor reference is checked and throws an error if not available before calling any actor method; 2) the correct actor method is invoked (e.g., actor.createClient, actor.createPart, etc.); 3) the argument shape exactly matches the backend interface signature (refer to backend.d.ts types). Fix any mutation that is a no-op, calls the wrong method, or passes incorrect arguments. Ensure all mutations return the expected result or void as appropriate.
